config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Inverted cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks English, US persona speaks Indonesian.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Inverted cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks English, US persona speaks Indonesian.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: DISAGREE. I believe individual rights should not be sacrificed just because society claims a greater interest, because that can easily become an excuse for abuse by the majority or by the state. In my view, a healthy society protects basic rights first, then works out the common good within those limits. Social interests matter, but they should never automatically override the dignity and freedom of each person.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus didahulukan atas hak individu secara umum, karena di Amerika saya melihat betapa mudahnya “demi kepentingan bersama” dipakai untuk membenarkan penyalahgunaan kuasa. Hak individu bukan penghalang bagi masyarakat; justru itu pagar yang mencegah mayoritas atau pemerintah menginjak minoritas. Tentu ada situasi darurat seperti kesehatan publik atau keamanan, tetapi pembatasan hak harus sangat sempit, jelas, dan bisa dipertanggungjawabkan, bukan jadi aturan default. Kalau hak individu selalu dikalahkan, yang tersisa bukan masyarakat yang sehat, melainkan masyarakat yang patuh.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The interests of society should not automatically take priority over the rights of the individual, because that is exactly how abuse gets justified in the name of order or harmony. From an Indonesian perspective, I have seen how people can use “the common good” to pressure others into silence, and that makes me wary of any argument that treats individual rights as secondary by default. Social needs do matter in things like public health or disaster response, but those limits must be narrow, transparent, and temporary. A society is not truly strong if it only looks united on the surface while ignoring the dignity and freedom of the people inside it.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak pernyataan itu sebagai prinsip umum, karena di pengalaman Amerika saya, alasan “demi masyarakat” terlalu sering dipakai untuk membenarkan kontrol yang berlebihan dan menekan kelompok minoritas. Kepentingan bersama memang penting, tetapi ia harus dibatasi oleh hak dasar individu; kalau tidak, mayoritas bisa dengan mudah mengorbankan orang yang paling lemah. Saya baru bisa menerima prioritas kepentingan masyarakat dalam keadaan darurat yang nyata, dan itu pun harus sempit, sementara, serta diawasi ketat. Bagi saya, masyarakat yang sehat bukan yang paling patuh, melainkan yang tetap melindungi kebebasan orang-orang di dalamnya.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I reject this statement as a general rule, because in Indonesia I have seen how “society” can be used as a moral shield for pressure, conformity, and even abuse. Social interests do matter, especially in emergencies like public health or disaster response, but they should not automatically outrank basic individual rights. If the majority can override dignity whenever it claims to be acting for the common good, then the weakest people are the first to lose protection. A healthy society is not one that silences individuals for the sake of harmony, but one that protects rights while still pursuing shared goals.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak pernyataan itu sebagai prinsip umum, karena dari sudut pandang Amerika, “kepentingan masyarakat” terlalu mudah dipakai untuk membenarkan pelanggaran hak oleh mayoritas atau pemerintah. Hak individu bukan lawan dari masyarakat; justru hak itu yang mencegah masyarakat berubah menjadi alat penindasan terhadap orang yang paling lemah. Saya mau memberi prioritas terbatas pada kepentingan publik hanya dalam keadaan darurat yang benar-benar nyata, dan itu pun harus sempit, sementara, serta diawasi ketat. Kalau tidak, yang disebut kebaikan bersama sering kali cuma jadi alasan rapi untuk memaksa orang patuh.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
